---
title: "Many Unserialize T Demo"
format: html
filters:
  - tlang
---

# Introduction

This demo showcases a T pipeline where different nodes use varying serialization formats (**Arrow**, **CSV**, **JSON**).

# Final Merged Dataset

The `mtcars_mpg` node is the result of a join of two nodes using different data formats.

```{t}
#| label: merged-data
# Node that uses many unserialization strategies
node_join = read_node("mtcars_mpg")
df_join = read_arrow(node_join.path)
df_join |> head(5)
```

# Mixed serialization details

- `mtcars_tail` was serialized as **JSON**.
- `mtcars_head` was serialized as **CSV**.

```{t}
#| label: tail-data
node_tail = read_node("mtcars_tail")
# read_json returns a T value (list of dicts usually for tables)
tail_data = t_read_json(node_tail.path)
print(str_join(["Tail data length: ", length(tail_data)]))
```

The T language handles the mapping between these diverse formats across the pipeline boundaries.
